name: safe_agentic_workflow
description: SAFe Agentic Workflow (SAW)-modeled software delivery pipeline: task intake -> Business Systems Analyst requirements gate -> author a task-specific coding workflow (.botcircuits/workflows/coding/<task>_workflow.json) -> run that coding workflow -> QAS validation gate with reroute authority (capped at 3 cycles) -> Release Train Engineer PR shepherd (never fixes code, escalates instead) -> System Architect design review -> automated review pass -> human approval/merge, each rejection path routed back through the coding workflow with the specific findings -> documentation summary.

variables.description: The feature or task the user wants implemented, as originally provided.
The derived, testable acceptance criteria / definition of done for the task.
Whether the acceptance criteria are clear, complete, and testable.
Name of the task-specific coding workflow authored under .botcircuits/workflows/coding/ (e.g. user_login_workflow).
The specific, actionable defect findings (from QAS, the System Architect, automated review, or the human reviewer) the next coding-workflow run must fix. Empty on the first pass.
Number of QAS validation cycles run against the current implementation.
Whether QAS validated the implementation against acceptance_criteria.
Number of System Architect design-review cycles run.
Whether the System Architect approved the architecture/design of the implementation.
Number of automated (lint/style/consistency) review cycles run.
Whether the automated review pass found no blocking issues.
Whether the Release Train Engineer confirmed the PR is ready to move into review.

settings.action: Please describe the task you want implemented. Include: the feature or problem to solve, specific requirements, acceptance criteria if you already have them, and relevant context (tech stack, constraints, etc.).

You are a Business Systems Analyst. Review task_description (and any clarification provided since the last pass) and derive acceptance_criteria: a specific, testable definition of done. If the task description already contains clear acceptance criteria, formalize them; if requirements are missing, ambiguous, or too vague to derive testable criteria from, set requirements_complete to false and write down exactly what is missing or unclear. Otherwise set requirements_complete to true. Do not guess at missing requirements — only proceed when they are actually clear.

The task description is missing information needed to derive clear, testable acceptance criteria. Please answer the specific questions raised by the requirements review above so the acceptance criteria can be finalized.

You are the coding-workflow planner. Author the TASK-SPECIFIC coding workflow for this task, following the botcircuits-coding-workflow-authoring skill exactly. Derive a slug-safe task name from task_description (e.g. 'implement user login' -> user_login), write the workflow JSON to .botcircuits/workflows/coding/<task-name>_workflow.json with concrete, ordered implementation steps derived from acceptance_criteria (e.g. db table design -> backend db config -> API endpoint -> UI form -> tests), then build it with `botcircuits workflow build --name <task-name>_workflow`. If a coding workflow for this exact task already exists and is built, reuse it instead of re-authoring. Set coding_workflow_name to the workflow's name (e.g. user_login_workflow). Do NOT implement any application code in this step — only author and build the coding workflow.

Initialize workflow state for a fresh implementation pass: set qas_iteration to 0, qas_approved to false, architect_iteration to 0, architect_approved to false, automated_review_iteration to 0, automated_review_passed to false, pr_ready to false, and revision_findings to an empty string.

Execute the task-specific coding workflow by running: `botcircuits workflow run --name <coding_workflow_name> --initial-args '{"task_description": <task_description>, "acceptance_criteria": <acceptance_criteria>, "revision_findings": <revision_findings>}'` (JSON-escape the values; pass revision_findings as an empty string on the first pass). The coding workflow's own deterministic engine drives the implementation steps. Do NOT implement code yourself outside that run. When it finishes, report what was built or fixed and which acceptance criteria it addresses; if the run reports failure, state exactly what failed.

You are the Quality Assurance Specialist (QAS) and hold iteration authority over this pipeline. Increment qas_iteration by 1. Validate the implementation produced by the coding workflow against every item in acceptance_criteria. If every criterion is met, set qas_approved to true and clear revision_findings. Otherwise set qas_approved to false and set revision_findings to specific, actionable findings: which acceptance criterion failed, in which file/layer the defect lives, and what must change — precise enough that the coding workflow can fix exactly that defect on its next run.

You are the Release Train Engineer, acting as PR shepherd. You do NOT write, fix, or modify code. Summarize the changes made by the coding workflow runs, and confirm QAS sign-off from qas_approved. If qas_approved is true, set pr_ready to true and prepare a PR summary for review. If qas_approved is false (the QAS cycle cap was reached without approval), do not attempt to fix anything yourself — set pr_ready to false and write an escalation summary describing exactly which acceptance criteria remain unresolved and why.

The QAS validation cycle cap was reached without approval and the Release Train Engineer escalated rather than attempting a fix. Compile the QAS findings history and the Release Train Engineer's escalation summary into a single report describing the unresolved acceptance criteria, for a human to decide how to proceed.

You are the System Architect. Increment architect_iteration by 1. Review the implementation at the architecture/design level: component boundaries, integration correctness across the layers the coding workflow touched, consistency with existing system design, and technical debt introduced. This is distinct from QAS's acceptance-criteria-level review — evaluate structure and design quality, not just whether acceptance criteria pass. If the design is sound, set architect_approved to true. Otherwise set architect_approved to false and set revision_findings to specific findings describing what must change and where.

Run the automated review pass: lint, style, and cross-file consistency checks across all code changed by the coding workflow. Increment automated_review_iteration by 1. If no blocking issues are found, set automated_review_passed to true. Otherwise set automated_review_passed to false and set revision_findings to the specific blocking issues found.

The PR summary, QAS sign-off status, architecture review, and automated review results are above. Reply 'approve' to merge, or 'reject' with comments explaining what's wrong: say whether it's an implementation/coding defect, an acceptance-criteria (QAS-level) issue, or an architecture/design issue.

conditions.condition: human rejects citing an implementation defect
human rejects citing a coding defect
human rejects citing an acceptance criteria issue
human rejects citing a QAS-level issue
human rejects citing an architecture issue
human rejects citing a design issue

settings.action: Set revision_findings to the human reviewer's rejection comments. Reset qas_iteration to 0, qas_approved to false, architect_iteration to 0, architect_approved to false, automated_review_iteration to 0, automated_review_passed to false, and pr_ready to false, so the full validation pipeline re-runs after the coding workflow fixes the human-flagged defect.

Reset qas_iteration to 0, qas_approved to false, architect_iteration to 0, architect_approved to false, automated_review_iteration to 0, automated_review_passed to false, and pr_ready to false, so QAS re-validates against the human's acceptance-criteria feedback and downstream gates re-run.

Reset architect_iteration to 0, architect_approved to false, automated_review_iteration to 0, automated_review_passed to false, and pr_ready to false, so the System Architect re-reviews against the human's architecture feedback and downstream gates re-run.

You are a documentation agent. Write a comprehensive implementation summary document with these sections: 1) What Was Built — feature overview and all components delivered, 2) Coding Workflow — the task-specific coding workflow used (coding_workflow_name) and its step outline, 3) Architecture — how the implemented layers integrate, informed by the System Architect's findings, 4) Acceptance Criteria Checklist — confirm each item in acceptance_criteria and its status, 5) Setup and Deployment Instructions — step-by-step guide to run or deploy the implementation, 6) Review History — summarize every QAS finding and coding-workflow rerun, every architect finding, every automated review issue, and the human approval decision, 7) Future Improvements — recommended follow-up work. Ground every statement in acceptance_criteria and the actual code and findings produced during the pipeline.
